_meta.note: Sample output of the prose-extraction heuristic, applied by hand to 46 commands across core and modules. Demonstrates the auto/stub distribution and the kinds of structural detail that auto-extraction captures vs misses. All entries need a human pass before promoting to `verified`.

returns.summary: Whether the key was set, optionally returning the previous value with GET.

oneof.when: GET not specified, key was set

oneof.summary: Key was not set or not created.
Previous value of the key, or null if the key did not exist before SET.
oneof.note: Prose enumerates further sub-conditions per branch. Heuristic flattens them; verify if needed.

returns.summary: Value of the key, or null if it does not exist.

oneof.summary: Value of the key.

returns.summary: Number of keys that were removed.

resp2.summary: Number of keys that were removed.

returns.summary: Number of keys that exist from those specified as arguments.

resp2.summary: Number of keys that exist from those specified as arguments.

returns.summary: Value of the key after the increment.

resp2.summary: Value of the key after the increment.

returns.summary: Value of the key after decrementing it.

resp2.summary: Value of the key after decrementing it.

returns.summary: Length of the string stored at key, or 0 when the key does not exist.

resp2.summary: Length of the string stored at key, or 0 when the key does not exist.

returns.summary: Length of the string after the append operation.

resp2.summary: Length of the string after the append operation.

returns.summary: Type of the key, or `none` when the key does not exist.

resp2.summary: Type of the key, or `none` when the key does not exist.
resp2.note: Possible values include string, list, hash, set, zset, stream, none. Not enumerated by extractor.

returns.summary: Confirms the rename succeeded.
Whether the source key was copied.

resp2.summary: 1 if source was copied, 0 if not.

returns.summary: Whether the expiry was set.

resp2.summary: 1 if the timeout was set; 0 if the key doesn't exist or the operation was skipped.

returns.summary: Confirms the database was flushed.
Confirms the background save was started or scheduled.
Number of fields that were added.

resp2.summary: Number of fields that were added.

returns.summary: Value associated with the field, or null if missing.

oneof.summary: Value associated with the field.

returns.summary: Number of fields that were removed, excluding non-existing fields.

resp2.summary: Number of fields that were removed, excluding non-existing fields.

returns.summary: Field names of the hash, or empty if the key does not exist.

resp2.summary: Field names of the hash, or empty if the key does not exist.

returns.summary: Values of the hash, or empty if the key does not exist.

resp2.summary: Values of the hash, or empty if the key does not exist.

returns.summary: Values associated with the requested fields, in order. Missing fields are null.

items.summary: Field value, or null if the field is not in the hash.
items.note: Per-field nullability inferred — prose says 'in the same order as requested' but does not explicitly call out null for missing fields. Verify.

resp2.summary: Values associated with the requested fields, in order.

returns.summary: Cardinality of the sorted set, or 0 if the key doesn't exist.

resp2.summary: Cardinality of the sorted set, or 0 if the key doesn't exist.

returns.summary: Score of the member, or null if the member or key is missing.

oneof.summary: Score of the member (double-precision floating point) as a string.
Score of the member (double-precision floating point).

returns.summary: Whether the element is a member of the set.

resp2.summary: 1 if the element is a member of the set, 0 otherwise (including when the key does not exist).

returns.summary: Cardinality of the set, or 0 if the key does not exist.

resp2.summary: Cardinality of the set, or 0 if the key does not exist.

returns.summary: Length of the list after the push operation.

resp2.summary: Length of the list after the push operation.

returns.summary: Element at the index, or null if the index is out of range.

oneof.summary: Requested element.

returns.summary: Popped element(s), or null if the key does not exist.

oneof.summary: Value of the first element.
List of popped elements.

returns.summary: ID of the added entry, or null when NOMKSTREAM is set and the key does not exist.

oneof.summary: ID of the added entry (auto-generated or supplied).

returns.summary: Number of entries in the stream at the key.

resp2.summary: Number of entries in the stream at the key.

returns.summary: Stream entries with IDs matching the specified range.

resp2.summary: Stream entries with IDs matching the specified range.

items.note: Stream entry shape not described in source prose. Expected: [id, kv_array of fields]. Candidate for $ref to a shared `stream_entry` schema.

returns.summary: Encoding of the object, or null if the key doesn't exist.

oneof.summary: Encoding of the object.

returns.summary: Replication role and role-specific details.

resp2.summary: First element is the role name; remaining elements depend on role.

rest.note: Role-specific tail not enumerated in source prose. Expected: per-role shape — master returns [replication_offset, [replica_descriptor...]]; slave returns [master_ip, master_port, link_status, replication_offset]; sentinel returns [[monitored_master_name...]]. Use the verification script to capture each.

returns.summary: Server's current Unix time as seconds and microseconds.

prefixItems.name: seconds
prefixItems.note: Prose says 'Unix timestamp in seconds' — element type inferred as bulk_string (RESP2 array elements). Verify whether server emits as integer or string.

prefixItems.name: microseconds
prefixItems.summary: Microseconds component of the current second.

returns.summary: PONG or the supplied message.

oneof.summary: The provided argument echoed back.

returns.summary: The given string echoed back.

resp2.summary: The given string.

returns.summary: Number of keys in the currently-selected database.

resp2.summary: Number of keys in the currently-selected database.

returns.summary: Server statistics and configuration as a CRLF-delimited key/value text document.

resp2.summary: Map of info fields, one field per line as `<field>:<value>`, with `#`-prefixed section headers and blank lines.

resp3.summary: Map of info fields, one field per line as `<field>:<value>`, with `#`-prefixed section headers and blank lines.

returns.summary: Information and statistics about client connections.

resp2.summary: One line per client, space-separated `key=value` pairs.
resp2.note: Internal record format described informally in the CLIENT LIST page. Heuristic captures the wire-type but not the structured record schema.

returns.summary: Search results matching the query.

oneof.summary: First element is total results, followed by document IDs and their field-value pairs as arrays.
oneof.note: Item shape not explicitly enumerated — implicitly a tuple of [total_results, ...(id, fields)] with conditional WITHSCORES/WITHPAYLOADS/NOCONTENT modifiers not captured here.

items.note: Per-document map fields not enumerated by prose. Verify with resp_inspect.py.

returns.summary: Index information and statistics.

oneof.summary: Index information and statistics as field-value pairs.
oneof.note: Field set not enumerated by prose. Use resp_inspect.py to capture the actual keys.

oneof.summary: Index information and statistics as a map.
oneof.note: Field set not enumerated by prose. Use resp_inspect.py to capture the actual keys.

returns.summary: Distinct tag values for the named field.

oneof.summary: Distinct tag values as bulk strings.
Distinct tag values as a set of bulk strings.

returns.summary: JSON-encoded value at the path(s).

resp2.summary: Serialised JSON. Single path → the value's serialisation. Multiple paths → a JSON object mapping each path to an array of serialisations.
resp2.note: Content is a JSON document; structure inside the string varies per query and is not captured at the RESP level.

resp3.summary: Serialised JSON. Single path with `$` → array containing the value. Multiple paths → object mapping each path to an array of serialisations.
resp3.note: RESP3 differs from RESP2 in that a single `$` path returns an array wrapper, not the raw value.

returns.summary: Confirms the path was set, or null/error when conditions are unmet.

oneof.when: path missing and uncreatable, or NX/XX condition unmet

messages.value: ERR new objects must be created at the root
messages.summary: Key does not exist and path is not root.
Dynamic path expression has no matching locations.
Path refers to an array index outside the array bounds.
One or more values of a FP array are out of range for the given type.

returns.summary: Time-series samples in the requested range.

oneof.summary: List of (timestamp, value) pairs.
List of (timestamp, value) pairs.

returns.summary: Whether the item was added to the Bloom filter.

oneof.summary: 1 if added, 0 if probably already present.
true if added, false if probably already present.

returns.summary: Whether the item probably exists in the Bloom filter.

oneof.summary: 1 if probably in the filter, 0 if definitely not (or key missing).
true if probably in the filter, false if definitely not (or key missing).
